core_mechanism: a codimension-two switch in the criticality of a Hopf-type onset of self-sustained oscillations, in which a secondary control parameter flips the birth of a limit cycle between a continuous (supercritical) and an abrupt, hysteretic (subcritical) transition, with the crossover mediated by a canard explosion — a fast-slow trajectory whose amplitude grows explosively yet continuously across a narrow parameter window
brief_reason: The abstract centers on a secondary parameter changing the transition to an oscillatory state from supercritical to subcritical Hopf bifurcation (change of criticality) and a 'metamorphosis' from continuous to abrupt onset occurring through a canard explosion.
